note: Field Formatter Class adds a single 'class' third-party setting to every field formatter/widget and renders it onto the field wrapper. It has no config route, no permissions, no services. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded): they probe documented facts an agent would otherwise read the .module source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site with Field Formatter Class enabled, where do I add a CSS class to a single field's output wrapper, and do I need any per-field configuration to turn the feature on first?
evals.expected_output: In Manage display (or Manage form for widgets), click the field's gear/edit icon and fill the 'Field Formatter Class' textfield. No per-field setup is needed to enable it — the setting appears on every formatter automatically.

evals.prompt: How is the Field Formatter Class value stored on the entity view display? Give the config structure.
evals.expected_output: As a third-party setting on the display config: third_party_settings.field_formatter_class.class = '<classes>'. It exports/imports with the display config; there is no separate config object.

evals.prompt: Which Drupal hooks does the field_formatter_class module implement to add its 'class' textfield to every formatter and to render the class onto the field?
evals.expected_output: hook_field_formatter_third_party_settings_form() adds the 'class' textfield; hook_field_formatter_settings_summary_alter() shows the summary; hook_preprocess_field()/hook_preprocess() append the class to the field wrapper's attributes.

evals.prompt: How does the configured class end up on the rendered field? Which template/attribute receives it and how is the string processed?
evals.expected_output: preprocess reads the stored class, runs it through Token::replace() (clear => TRUE), splits on spaces, Html::escape()s each, and appends to $variables['attributes']['class'], i.e. the field template (field.html.twig) wrapper div.

evals.prompt: Can the Field Formatter Class value contain tokens, and what makes a token browser link appear next to the setting?
evals.expected_output: Yes — the value passes through the token service (Token::replace scoped to the field's entity type), so entity tokens resolve per entity. If the Token module is installed, a token_tree_link browser link is shown next to the field.

evals.prompt: What modules does field_formatter_class depend on, and does it define any permissions or an admin configuration route?
evals.expected_output: It depends only on core's 'field' module. It defines no permissions and no configure route — the class setting simply appears on every field's formatter/widget settings.

evals.prompt: If I type multiple class names into the Field Formatter Class textfield, how are they applied to the field wrapper?
evals.expected_output: Space-separated values are split (explode on ' ') into individual classes, each HTML-escaped and added separately to the wrapper's class attribute.
